reviews.review_text:
Don't listen to the haters who claim this game is dead. The Devs do monthly updates and are constantly active on their discord. This game is absolutely a passion project and something that most casuals ignore is that the only reason we get to even help the devs with this early access playtest is because we BEGGED them to let us play it. Is it buggy? Yes. Is it clunky at times? Yes. Does my performance randomly tank sometimes to less than 15 FPS? Yes. 
B U T! I can look past all that because beneath the bugs and the performance issues is a game that in todays day and age is truly loved. Is truly cared for by both the devs who create and code for it, as well as the players who are truly invested in the lore and world that fundog is trying to build. I am one of the players that this caters to, I'm in love with the lore and the story this world is building. I cannot get enough and I will keep playing this till I die.

17 Hours of Forever Winter.

What can I say, overall I enjoyed playing the game. For once I was able to play a fully 3D extraction shooter that's only PVE. The fact that you're a scav stuck in a 3-way fight between factions mean guns aren't aiming at you unless you're asking for it. Or if you're forced into a corner or chokepoint with no alternative but to shoot the enemies that will otherwise go after you. It scratches that sneaky stealth game itch and the inner loot goblin in one go. 

While you're doing your damnest to stay out of the line of fire, and keeping yourself from provoking the armies, the very nice bonus of this game truly shines, which is to watch brutal cinematic fight scenes unfold around you. It was a spectacle watching a tank get ripped in half by hulking Euruskan Medium Mechs while I was waiting for an opening to run inside the extraction point inside the pipe. Or that one time I was out collecting loot and I happened upon a small squad of 3 Europan infantry get swarmed by Eurasian terminator cyborgs. Or sit back in awe as I witness a sniper and his spotter pick off key enemy infantry units one by one. 

But with the good, comes the inconveniences. First and foremost, the Prestige system. I wasted so many matches basically losing XP because I didn't know that levelling up wasn't automatic. I had to click the button level up first before I can gain experience for the next level. A possible fix for this is some notification somewhere telling the players that a character has reached max XP so they can do the manual levelling up if the game devs are inclined to keep this design. This way, the players won't feel like they wasted so much time just playing the game and enjoying the spectacles forgetting that they could have levelled up their characters for better performance while doing so. Also, not carrying over progress for the skills earned is a weird design. "Trading off" acquired perks for an overall boost in basic stats doesn't exactly make sense to me especially if they haven't reached level 50 yet in prestige. When I spent XP on perks, only to lose them when I ranked my prestige up tells me that investing on perks is pointless before I reach max prestige of 50. 

Next entry for inconvenience, "base building" and upgrading the Innards. It's hard to keep track on what items I need for a certain base upgrade I want to get. There isn't a checklist for players where they can set the construction for an Innards upgrade as a quest or a note somewhere in the game's UI to remind us what we're missing and what we should collect for base upgrades. I need to write a sticky note in real life to tell me that the TV upgrade needs 3 LCDs, 5 wires, and a bunch of other components. Besides the TV, I wanted to make a repair bench too to hopefully fix all these broken weapons I've been picking up from my runs. Zero Sievert does a good job at this part, where I can set a base upgrade as a quest, and I have a tracker on my UI to help me keep track of what I'm missing for upgrades.

There's a bunch of other painful experiences in this game but I'll end this review with this. I found it particularly inconvenient how rigs and the gun vendors are locked behind 11 and 16 water. Personally, that felt like a long grind to fetch the buckets of water in Ashen Mesa run after run. I was still fresh into the game and because I was thinking, if I shoot one soldier everyone else nearby will start shooting me instead I just kept running and running and almost never used my guns. I was happy that when I opened the donation box, I got a PPSh SMG, only to lose it in one of the runs in Ashen Mesa. But what else did I expect? This is an extraction shooter, losing your treasure because you failed to survive is exactly what the gameplay experience is. Frankly, the journey to 11 water was met with a lot of deaths and regrets.

Yeah, this review was 2 paragraphs of good, and 3 paragraphs of bad. But it's in early access, and so far, despite the pains, I still had a fun time playing this game. I'd keep playing but only on occasion since the pain of losing your gear, and your earnings each run because of skill issues can reach a certain limit. An older review was right. This was better approached as an artbook than the common game.

It's a cool idea for a game and I've had some fun runs with other players once, but only once.  I don't know how to actually find other people to play this, 99.999999999999999% of "lobbies" you will find are people just AFK at home base.  And then there are still tons of huge debilitating glitches and bugs.  I support the idea of buying this game just to support interesting creative devs, but this game still needs a bunch more work, so buyer beware.
It's not for everyone, in fact I'd say you gotta be a specific kinda person to like a game like this, though I cant really describe it

Judging this game by an aesthetic: 10/10
Judging this game by how fun it is to play: 2/10
It is very rarely rewarding, and usually a run ends up being a huge waste of time. It definitely has potential, but as it is now there is little reason to play other than the cool art and concepts.


Two things that I think (in my unprofessional opinion) should be fixed/changed in order to make this game wayyyy more enjoyable:

- It's okay for the game to be hard, but sometimes the AI focuses on you way more than it should, making it impossible to progress if you get unlucky. Ultimately, it feels entirely unrealistic that these grizzled, war-maddened soldiers would turn their entire unit's attention away from the conflict at hand and mow down my sorry ass rifling through a garbage pile nearby. The AI wander without any real sense of what they're supposed to be doing, sometimes literally walking in circles in the same dark room and never leaving. Making the enemies feel realistic and less dull would be a huge improvement, and I think there needs to be some big changes to the stealth mechanics.

- Overly complicated and punishing mission system. It makes sense why the developers would want to add a cost to travelling to different locations in a game trying to make you feel like a little scavenger scurrying around in a big big world, but as the game is now it grinds the gameplay loop to a halt (at least for me). Having to either trek through repetitive auto-shuffling tunnels to even have a chance to get to the place I want to explore is exhausting after the first few tries. You could spend your water resource, but you also need a drone thing(?) now for some reason. The current system just feels unnecessary and more importantly un-fun.

Fun Dog devs, if you're reading this (unlikely but pls do), I really like the idea of this game becoming something that's both awesome to look at as well as fun to interact with. It is absolutely possible, and I'm glad to see that you are willing to change around the gameplay mechanics to try and see what works. Please don't give up, I think Forever Winter could be an amazing game.

The gameplay is interesting but the leveling process is quite cumbersome: you lose all your unlocked perks each time you level up. So you spend most of your time without the upgrades you want until you're max level. And max level for a character is easily 100+ missions.

The game's atmosphere was definitely the main selling point for me. Everything around you from the landscapes, buildings to the infrastructure and facilities perfectly suits the vision of a torn world where our character tries to survive by gathering water and other supplies to improve our base and survivability by modifying our arsenal. "The end of the world" theme fits the game perfectly and supported strongly thanks to its unique artistic and design choices.

There have been lots of improvements since the game was released especially in terms of enemy behaviour, combat, movement, map design but our objective is still the same, don't engage with the enemy(preferably), get what you need,  extract safely,  complete missions to earn credit & reputation and repeat. Some might think it might get repetitive over time but It does not have one huge map where players fight with each other to survive, there are so far 9 unique maps to explore and it's not one of your typical battle royale or extraction shooter. You can play with up to 3 people or alone just fine. 

The game is not dead if anybody is wondering at least not yet but I can't say the same thing for its player base because player count is diminishing day by day. The devs roll out updates regularly but I'm not sure how long they can keep it up without seeing an increase in player count.

Forget what came out a year ago, this is like an entirely new game.

Rat King, Toothy, Europan medium mechs, Euraskan medium mechs: I love you and forgive you for stepping on me. You do have issues shooting at anything above you however. I have lost count of times i have played witness to one of these hulking beauts be brought low by helicopters, drones, even troops shooting at them from walkways.

How can a game like this... be clunkier than DayZ?

It has potential and it seems it's been receiving frequent updates, which is fantastic! Atmosphere alone is not going to save this project tho. I can't recommend it unless u wanna suffer a bit. I don't care if it has 20fps or if the AI pops up right in front of me. What the game lacks is proper "games design".

The BASIC elements are broken. You can keep your identity and still make use of great design practices that countless of other games have been evolving the past 30 year.

It desperately needs some networking fixes. I can barely load the game now, and the only time I have on this was from a significantly worse computer.

I don't make this review because I want to "review bomb". I want this game to do well - It's truly inspired, and I hope to love it fully for what it will become when it is ready. This negative review is here to say: Get the game to support the creators for now, enjoy it when it is complete. Have patience.

It's a diamond in the rough. For the fans, it's something really special. The experience continues to evolve and I can't wait to see where we end up.

The art and the world of The Forever Winter are stunning. There's really nothing quite like it.

For now, I'd wait on it if you're unsure. For me, I wanted to experience the world the devs are building and it's been worth my $30.  There's a lot of work to be done for the moment-to-moment to become more fun. 

One of the biggest issues to me is that the battlefields aren't fully simulated except in the area around the player. It kind of makes the fantasy of crawling through a hellscape while a massive war unfolds around you feel hollow. The game is undeniably tense, though. I just wouldn't expect to sink your teeth into deep systems and mechanics.

I'm absolutely going to update my review as soon as I feel like I can recommend it. I'm rooting for Fundog because I've seen how passionate they are and how good they are at communicating with the community.

Honestly, ♥♥♥♥ this game.

I've lost my fav LMG to a game crash, drowned in knee deep water in a frozen swamp, gotten trapped in a tunnel by my trusty pack mule who doesn't respect personal space, fallen countless times to my death due to ♥♥♥♥♥♥ platforming, and still I come back for more.

What is wrong with me?

I've owned this game for 24 hours and have at this moment played it for 11 hours.  Its buggy, it crashes, there are people who depend on you "We need water." Terminator is on your left, Mechs are on your right, Tanks are behind, and Drones are in front of you. Its not as long, hard, and sand papery as lets say Tarkov, but it does give you the right amount of hope. Its only gonna tear that hope away because you brought only 40 00 Buckshot rounds with you. I'm going to keep going but unless you are looking for a SOLID game that has the best aspects from the others all smashed into one, pick another one. "But Richard, it doesn't have PvP!" F'n great. I hope it stays like that. I don't want to deal with all the aforementioned BS as well as pole smoking cheaters. All of that said, I look forward becoming a god in this game. "Richard! You can't do that!" 1. Not with that attitude you can't. 2. Stand back, hold my beer, and watch this.

people hate games that give you a real challenge or a learning curve so ignore the ragequitting comments. its a grindy game for sure and the menus are a little more confusing than most games but when you learn the system it beats arc raiders. the biggest problem with this game is accessibility with menus. Everything else is better than it was 2 years ago

Enemy AI hasn't improved at all. There's more boring and confusing loot and the crafting system looks worse than arc raiders. Tunnels are boring. You can still cheese AI around the corner, they will always storm the front in a line. Maybe focus on that? Watersystem isn't any better now, just get rid of it, it's not immersive in this clanky shooter. Kill a squad? You'll get swarmed in under a minute. It was so great when it came into early access, but the progress doesn't point into a direction I like.

The art and world building are some of the best i've seen but the game it self feels floaty and boring! I've been keeping my eye on this since it first released in EA and i finally gaev in and bought it but what a dissapointment it turned out to be :( I feel like soldiers and mechs spot you waaaay to quickly especially the mechs who shouldn't care much about some hobo looking scavenger when there's a full squad of enemies on the other side of the trench shooting at it.
wait till they finish the game and even then id say hold your breath went in to this game with high hopes but after 2 matches the early access rlly starts to show pretty bare bones to be honest

It's a very promising game that currently gets hard carried by the art team.
That being said, once it has more gameplay diversity and is significantly more polished, it has the potential to be something really special. They seem to drop patches and updates pretty regularly, which gives me a lot of hope for the game's future.

Worth buying on sale, but probably not for the full price, at least not yet.
I rate it 6/10 currently with the potential to be a 9/10 game in a couple years.

Despite all the gameplay jank and less broadly appealing gamplay design direction, even if you dislike the game it's almost worth the 30EUR for the world and visuals alone. There's artbooks sold for more than 30EUR already. There are no games and barely any other media i've seen that has such an amazing and uncompromisingly realized style. Just wandering around some maps for the first time is an unforgettable experience. Sadly if this is all you're after recent changes to map selection has made this slightly more difficult.

The gameplay is hard to talk about because it is intentionally jank and uncomfortable to reinforce the way the developers want you to play. After all if it had responsive CoD style movement and shooting you would seem less like a lowly scav, and if you balanced the enemies to compensate the game get an unreasonably high barrier of entry for anyone not already skilled in shooters. The compromise is that the game feels slightly awful to play for everyone. Shooting is not responsive or precise, with your aim lagging behind your cursor and weapons often having a very large spread. Movement is slow and equally unresponsive, mostly manegable unless the game wants you to parkour. to exxagurate it feels like you are telling your character what to do and then waiting until he's processed and acted on it, there's a slight lag to it and in tense situations you cant trust it.

i don't care much about the progression of the game so cant comment much. All i know is that it's reasonably long but finite. When you're done there's nothing to do but to just keep playing and hoping the core gameplay can entretain you. 

The performance isn't stellar but a 4070 super can at least run it above 60 at 1440p without upscaling.
There's no AA besides upscalers or TAA so the game always looks slightly blurry and ghosty. Most annoyingly there's no option to turn off AA entirely, even with commands in the unreal engine config file, so you are forced to have it blurry.

The game is unfinished and still being developed. The most recent update shows they're at least aware of common player complaints and will work to fix them, so with some luck the finished game will have the bugs ironed out and the gameplay perhaps improved in a way that satisfies the dev's vision while also being more fun to play.

oh boy were to start, I personally don't have much issues with the game being still being bare bones and asking you to mindlessly grind at certain points of progression since I am having faith that fundog will build it out further. The AI still feels unpredictable at times although I am having way more neutral encounters even if I am spotted which is very nice to have that special standoff with a soldier that riloe talked about before the game was in open beta. My main complaint and reason I still don't recommend it is optimization, I get that fundog is small but I personally would be ashamed to use UE5 the way that they have been, using nanite and ai tools as crutches, it is literally the worst running game I have played so far. I'd like to point to clair obscure as an example of good optimization of UE5 from another very small indie dev.
The battles, the weapons... THE LOOT! Truly an amazing game, from start to finish. Atmosphere like no other game, ever. FOREVER.

I've played a decent amount of Forever Winter and I have read the reviews and honestly I think this game gets a lot of flak it doesn't deserve, now I'm going to be clear- It's not perfect, It's very much early access but I have seen imo consistant positive changes to the game and I honestly think its really fun and the setting is awesome. The most recent few updates for me have been good changes and I've been enjoying the new progression. 

I think my take generally is that I think it is worth trying but you have to go into it knowing its early access and certain things are somewhat rough and I know that some people's PCs struggle to run the game but personally I'm on CachyOS with a RTX4070 and a i7-13700KF and I have had no problems at all with my FPS only dropping below 72 in a single map.

If you are worried about the state/quality of the game or if your PC can run it or not: My advice would be to wait a year or two and then look back at it but for me at least I've been having a blast and will continue to play and support the developers.

I don't have any ill will towards this game, I don't hate it outright, but its a very repetitive gameplay loop if you want to consistently make money, If you only run the surplus ammo, and have the rig that lets you just carry big items you can get 50-80k each run just by grabbing water and lootboxes, the concept is really cool being a future dystopian war ridden wasteland hell, constantly warring factions that are all unique but it unfortunately suffers from being an extraction shooter, maybe I'm just biased against the short form entertainment. The old water mechanic was better for player retention needing to have a constant supply or risk losing all of your progress, but was to harsh for people that would stop playing for a day or two, it should've been more balanced, a fix could've been only ticking down the water clock when the player was on the game, or taking water if the mission was lost.
Stealth? Nah we'll just put enemies right in your path which you can't get around. Genius. ♥♥♥♥ this game. I want to love it so bad man but it's got balancing problems.

Downtown LA, hiding in the rubble as a constellation of hunter killers float overhead, I was struck by the beauty of a moment. We were cavemen again, looking out at the ancient monsters from the safety of our shallow hole. It was vivid and surreal, a painting I could move inside of, but didn't dare. A trespasser in a diorama. I've never felt this way in any other media.

If you have the patience for the lagging and bugs, this game could be good. I do enjoy playing, some maps I would go to, while I try to avoid others such as the Stairway gate and elephant mausoleum because of instant lag and bugs.

This game is fantastic. Post apocaliptic survival porn. Not the soldier, but the people caught inbetween. I love the direction its going. I like the risk. I hate cyborgs. All the guns are really satisfying after this last patch. I think its work buying as a non-commital, pick it up - put it down, dopamine drip.

2 years later, Servers are still Dogwash the gun play is poorly and lazily made, The animations are buggy. The tunnels are the worst thing added to this game. And the devs just dont seem to care or listen to the players, Other then changing the water due to cry babys.

[b]Fundog lied on multiple occasions, but I'm still hoping it was over-ambition and/or inexperience, not greed.[/b]

[i]Edit, for transparency: I've tried playing the game across a few different systems with unfortunately similar results.[/i]

If even half the reviews here are to be believed, plenty of us are quite passionate about this game's overall theme and setting - even moreso after the beautiful promises and marketing videos we all saw - so I imagine it's hard not to feel at least a little let down.

Yes, "It's early access" and "bugs are to be expected" and "they only let us play it because we [i]begged[/i] them to," etc. I've heard and read plenty of that, but I do not believe these are valid excuses - here's why:
[list]
[*] For a long time, the game was almost completely (now upgraded to "often") unplayable for many people, due to a zesty combination of technical issues and baffling design choices. Call me crazy, but paying for a game should at least get us a playable build, even if it's severely unpolished - yes, even in EA. True, the particularly impiatient among us were even louder than usual, but nobody truly forced Fundog to overpromise, to under-deliver, or to be impatient themselves.
[*] There are clear financial incentives for EA, especially with the way the game is currently structured. In a typical playthrough, your first minutes in the "main" gameplay loop are well outsdie the refund window. I don't currently believe this was done maliciously, but it still leaves a bad taste.
[*] Technical issues aside, the game is simply not what was advertised. If we're meant to be playing as "not that guy" and just be sneaky, insignificant scum, heavy mech/tank units probably shouldn't stop fighting each other just to attack us like we insulted their RGB gaming chair. Being punished for mistakes and dying often is fine on its own (f♥♥♥ knows I'm used to it, lol), but the feeling of dying unfairly lasts. [spoiler](inb4 "skill issue")[/spoiler] On that note, I'm still not sure where we're meant to land on the "Coughing Baby -- Space Marine Without a Helmet" power scale, come late-game.
[*] When we do [strike]respawn[/strike] make it back to base, we encounter some of the more obnoxious mechanics from mobile games and the likes of Tarkov -- even though Fundog has been saying since their earliest promo that they "get it" and will actively avoid all that. This one stings, so I'm really hoping it's more of an experimental throwing-stuff-at-the-wall situation, rather than a sign of microtransactions to come.
[/list]

[b]With the salt and bile out of my system, and with all my callous little gamer heart, I truly hope to one day eat my words and update this review to Positive.[/b]

The game is fine for Early Access; it has a lot of things to polish, enemy spawn, map generation, vendor trust, and especially the war AI. You can't even think of entering the last areas alone, enemies home-in straight towards you if they catch a sight of your shoulder, leg, knee, head or rig, prompting a firefight to begin almost immediately. And no, you won't win, they have more ammo and health, and you're a scav condemned to a few rounds, some medkits, and having the armor of an ant.

I'mma leave a positive review because I really love the game, and it would be amazing to see it succeed.

The devs dont know what direction they going i guess,they say youre not that guy but they literally give options to make a railgun to kill armored enemy,and much of our quest was going to kill bunch of enemies rather than hide and retrieve stuff.Devs make great lore but bad game direction tbh,at current version theres alot need to fix,i cant recommend but if you insist to try it you need to know what youre getting into,so research at own will.

[h3] a huge Recommendation from my side. [/h3] 

This game throws you into a world engulfed in war between two factions...
and you are on neither side.
They dont care for you, at best you are a disposable convenient tool at the moment, discarded at the next.
survival is as much skill as it is "luck".
stand at the wrong moment at the wrong time, and you will get cought in the criossfire.
and yet, you will have to venture out again and again, for the tools of survival are found on the active battlefields.

The game manages to find that sweet spot between uncaring for you, the player, and fair.
If you die, its because you ddint pay enough attention to your surroundings when making decisions.
You are rewarded equally for good preparation and for the ability to adapt on the fly, and it makes for a most amazing combination.

Graphically there are stil a few things that feel meh, but it truly nails the "world gone to hell" feeling.
The audiodesign and soundtrack are amazing, underlining the atmosphere created by your surroundings.

The story is... there. its getting expanded upon in recent updates, and is being told in lore snippets during quests, and by the world itself.

If you are looking for an extraction Shooter that hits the note of "you are a nobody" but also "you can make a difference for yourself", pick it up, you wont be disappointed.
Game dev totally broke the game for linux to a point is unplayable, no fix i've seen / found helped to solve the problem... waiting for the next patch to fix this mess... very annoying

I usually don't like extraction shooters at all but I keep trying them and for some reason, this one clicks.

Dark Souls Tombstone mechanic and economy are great. Very easy to buy yourself back. Paying water for fast travel makes sense and gives you multiple paths for risk/reward. Solo play is fine, maybe more difficult on later levels. Solo Scav runs can take 4 minutes and you have enough for AK, ammo, and backpack. 

Art is fantastic. Some very cool looking characters and environments that makes running the same level over and over not so bad.

The main issue I have with the game is the gunplay, player movement, enemy visibility and AI. If everything was like in MGS V, this game would be a 9/10 as it is right now. Movement is not bad but like a worse version of Remnant. The gameplay begs to let you dive and roll and crawl.

The gunplay gets better as you go along, but will obviously be rebalanced I assume when a .45 USP can headshot but an AK cannot. I like what they're going for but stock FOV and HUD make some sights really hard to use. I have to use reshade to map capslock to kill the HUD so I can try to headshot whatever is trying to kill me. Too much BS on the screen. If it's going to be cyberpunk why can't it be helpful? Why does it tag enemies after aiming at them for a few seconds then immediately untag them? That would be handy to know where my enemies are when I'm trying to sneak around them.

Finally the AI. Some good, some bad and some ugly here. The best is what you see on the store page and on the trailers. Constant infighting. Close air support. They regularly carpet bomb you with a dozen Naruto-running Terminators (apparently this is to disguise a spawner, very cool and clever!). You will get into gunfights, and stealth kill someone, and throw a couple cluster bombs into a platoon. You will hide from drones and mechs and loot containers as you pac-man through patrols, trenchgunning the couple unlucky fools in your way and one drone that looked at you funny. When things work, they work and there is a game here. A good game.

But with that being said, there are more than a few caveats that kept me away from the game until now. The AI is "braindead" sometimes, and hacking at others. That's a gross oversimplification, but that's how it feels.

Once you start putting the hours into the game you realize that the AI is just playing by its own rules that are not like stealth games at all. More like Terrorist Hunt in Rainbow 6 Vegas. They don't aggro until they do and you better put them down or get away fast.

Sometimes you die to BS. I had 1 crash in 18 hours and I had my stuff in my inventory like the run never started. 

Performance was ok but 1% lows were bad. 
Framegen ingame fixed it completely and doesn't really play any different. This is probably the only shooter I am okay with using framegen on.

Overall not bad with friends, fun solo, cool experience and good to grind away at. Not sure it can be a 400-1200 hour game like Tarkov at the moment but with polish and 1.0 it could be a 2000 hour game for me. Love the focus on PvE and the current updates/direction of the game. I'd 100% recommend at sale price and I'm looking forward to the next updates!

game is fun af basically a pve of arc but more grim , its hard and i think thats why ppl are leaving negative reviews too many peeps want games to hold thier hands i hope devs dont make it easier to placate the whiners

very pre-alpha build. It has been awhile in early access and plagued by same issues from release. Every change made by dev make game more punishing but not in a good challenging way. Near impossible solo. unoptimized and not thought out mechanics that just add needles grind. Maybe good in 3 to 5 years.

The gameplay is nice. You are not a hero, but with some planing and lvls, you can deal with pretty much everything. Maybe that i would recommend to wait for a little discount since the game is somewhat bare for the moment, but as you can see with my playtime, even at full price you will have good value. The gunplay remind me of old CounterStrike in the sens that you need to focus on headshots more then anything else. Most of the time, one bullet is enough and spam clicking is way more accurate then holding the trigger. 

The cons of the game, for me, are mostly optimisation and colision with the decor. Sometime you get stuck on invisibles things that you need to jump on, sometime you walk on invisibles stuff on the floor which break the immersion by making you look like you walk on air, sometime you fall through things that ressemble solid ground into water and die (oh frozen swamp, I hate you. It mostly happened to me on that map), but most of the time, when that happen, you can somewhat blame yourself because you clearly tried to take an unintended path.
The game had a cool concept but it just never got finished... way too many bugs not worth your time

While this game has an incredible setting and artwork, the overall gameplay is rotten to it's core. All Movement, weapon-handling, economy, and AI is just terrible. I come back every now and then to check if one of the frequent patches made it more enjoyable - but no major progress has been made on those core aspects of the game, and I am starting to loose hope that it ever will.

Game crashes constantly. Until optimisation is done cannot recommend. Had fun with the recent changes but constantly unable to play. Made changes to drivers and settings but nothing appears to resolve the issue. Game still has loads of potential. Hope it gets optimised at some point.

I like this game. It has cool ideas and a fun gameplay loop. The art and music and story and visual design is incredible. However, its very buggy and has significant performance issues at the moment.

While most of the game runs fine, explosions will tank FPS to a near freeze for a second. I managed to fix this by following an online guide for creating a file called "Engine.ini" to adjust the lighting engine so this stops happening.
Sometimes your camera is ripped away for a few seconds. Not sure what causes this.

A lot of the pain points in this game come down to a lack of giving the player the proper information to do what they need to do. There is no map, there is no quest markers for things like theater of war objectives or radars. There is no way to tell what item comes from where, or where a specific enemy may be found. Despite different enemies in the same category having unique loot drops, pinging them will just give back the category name.
And so for a new player, its easy to get lost and not know what you're supposed to do. And online resources are not the best at the moment, especially when it comes to recently changed content.

Not quite the gameplay I expected based on developmental artwork, but it has become probably my favorite extraction shooter and I like the players.
I had issues with crashing on initial load, but it was solved as soon as I reinstalled nvidia drivers

That "Early Access Game" title might make you raise some eyebrows, but if you're willing to put up with some Early Access jank, then I cannot stress enough: [b] GRAB A COPY! [/b]

The Forever Winter is one of those games that comes along and is immediately appealing for its aesthetic and its interesting world, and all of the horrors that you'll find inside this twisted, macabre world. Now, I won't shy away from a very clear fact: [u] this game isn't finished. [/u] 
As a few other reviews point out, the game's map meshes and geometry really do leave a lot to be desired. You will occasionally get stuck on objects or you'll sometimes phase through something you didn't expect (looking at you, Injection Well) and you'll sometimes be left frustrated by this. The AI isn't particularly amazing either, and this is largely the result of what happens when a game is moreso driven by its art style, rather than it's coding and mechanics. That said, the mechanics that are there are solid in-terms of gun play; looting; character weight; UIs, and a few others I am likely forgetting.
So, I recommend this game for it's unique looking world and frankly rather, at least to [i]me[/i], game play loop.

But, if you're looking for an interesting extraction, looter shooter with a world that looks like it was pulled from the terrifying painting works of Zdzisław Beksiński? 
Then this will scratch that strange itch.
They made innards longer and more complex and  filled them with murderbots. Top tier game design.

I was playing having fun but when i came back after some time i discovered they limited the amount you can get to survive on the field and made a crafting system to get it or a quest which makes it seem like a lowgrade RPG like game instead of the loot/survive and shoot game. I stopped playing due to optimization being horrible and causing this system to crash even on the low settings/ which is pretty poorly done on it. Nice enemies but fix your issues fully.

It’s disappointing because the update was supposed to improve things, but instead it broke the game for me.

I hope the developers address this issue soon because right now, I can’t even get past the launch screen. Until then, I can’t recommend the game in its current state.

Playing The Forever Winter feels like being Chris Rock at the Oscars.
You show up expecting entertainment, maybe even a good time. Then out of nowhere the game walks up on stage and slaps you directly across the face. The difference is Chris Rock only got hit once.
The Forever Winter hits you every single time you log in.
The AI doesn't feel intelligent; it feels actively offended by your existence. The combat is less "tactical survival" and more "watching your hopes and dreams get vaporized by something you never saw coming." Every expedition feels like you're a background extra in a war movie whose sole purpose is to die horribly so the audience understands how dangerous the setting is.
The game constantly reminds you that you are not the hero. You're not even the side character. You're discarded battlefield debris with a pulse.
The controls can feel clunky, the progression can feel punishing, and the overall experience often feels less like survival and more like serving a court-ordered sentence. You spend hours scraping together resources only to lose everything because some mechanized nightmare spotted a single pixel of your elbow from three postal codes away.
The atmosphere is incredible, which is fortunate because you'll spend most of your time staring at it while dead.
The Forever Winter is the gaming equivalent of paying someone to repeatedly kick you in the balls and then thanking them for the opportunity.

Final Verdict: If Chris Rock's Oscar moment was an 8-second clip, The Forever Winter is the 40-hour extended edition. 1/10. The only thing surviving the apocalypse is my regret.

I won't copy the paragraphs of critique other reviews already have, the short of it is that they're right.

The game isn't fully realized yet, but despite that i find it to be an amazing play that's has me hooked, I only started two days ago and have already sunk almost 13 hours into it. It's an extremely unique title with amazing character and world design, honestly the gold standard in regards to creativity and identity. The visuals of an ongoing war is unmatched and scurrying amongst the carnage fills a specific niche I didn't know I had.

The gripes people have now are valid, but also the type of feedback the devs are looking for, and I think it's good that they want it! I remember being on the fence about this game when I first saw it last year, and it was in a significantly worse place. Many of those gameplay feature have been changed for the better like the offline, water drain being removed, and I don't doubt many of the criticisms people have now will be out of date a few updates later. 

If I had to offer my two cents on what actually needs improvement (since you can patch out features you don't like with mods) would be and overhaul to gunplay since the balance is all over the place. The default weapons, for example, are both OP and awful, with the starter shotgun able to clear most early game threats with cheap/light ammo, and the rifle handles like a immersive piece of garbage, but is only effective at ranges the shotgun can't kill, which is already far out of ever needing to engage with the enemy your trying to kill. Rifle balancing in general is really weak compared to simply getting any shotgun meatshot, perhaps a improvement to their recoil, to make them more useful at range and to promote more benefits compared to run and gunning with cqc. 
  Human NPC animations could also use a touchup, the giant mechs and vehicle are beautiful in their movements, which makes it jarring compared to how stiff the 2000's PS2 animations of the basic footsoldiers are. They basically move like Roblox NPC's for reference. Some of the player movement can also be silly, like hands positioning/movements and reloads being very simple in general.
  Also some variation in the map generation would be fun, I love how the tunnels actually get a new layout every irl day and think something like that would be great for the regions themselves, 

Tl;dr I think if you are considering buying, give it a shot, I believe it's worth the price tag, and as long as you understand what the store page is trying to tell you (play the scavenger, you will run and hide a lot in the early game, and as you progress, combat becomes more feasible yet still not a good idea) then I think you'll thoroughly enjoy.

it was fine when you could go to any map instantly and just look at the incredable art but they stacked so much unessasary ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ on top, i have a life ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, wasted a week of my life on this

It is a game with a lot of potential,. but it seems to have fallen off after initial hype.
Designwise I love it, the aesthetics and feel of it. But some of the gameplay needs more work and it really needs
more patching before it gets more stuff done to the shop....

Much improved compared to a year ago, currently early game but  I can see the SCAV fantasy this game is aiming for.

The game can be janky at times however the foundation of a extremely fun experience is there 

The quest and upgrades system always mean you have a clear idea of what you want and need to do, but also doesn’t overwhelm you.

The combat is surprisingly fun and encourages stealth gameplay early on but can award aggressive gameplay later on but this is also capped by certain maps being almost impossible to survive without late game stuff

The games economy is in a pretty well balanced spot

But the main thing I like about the game is the devs doing monthly update logs, they actively work on the game and have been getting better with the engine and mechanics

I think this is one of the most unique games in the last few years in terms of its atmosphere and art direction. Its the perfect blend of a post apocalyptic warzone with cyberpunk elements mixed together. Once you learn the ropes and step out of the underbelly of the world, you step into literally "hell on earth" as you explore the massive war zone that keeps you on your toes.

The sense of danger and chaos as you explore the world is a really fresh way of creating a world in a video game.

Sure, the game has countless bugs and issues that need to desperately be resolved, but hopefully this is achieved soon. I don't understand the negative reviews, I personally think this is one of the best "Extraction Shooters" out there and one that gives you such an epic and large scale feeling to a video game world.

Its a game with a lot of rough edges, but made with a lot of love.

Problems in question:
- Frame rate spikes and dives depending on the map and number of enemies on the map.
- The red-dots and some other scopes are very blurry, makes it hard to look down.
- Can cause your CPU to overheat, if not equipped with the proper specs.
- Although the tunnel system is neat, it feels rather brutal that the fast-travel system requires both a fast-travel drone and water. Would be better if you could choose between the two.

HOWEVER
I do very much love this game, despite its issues. The fact that I can play this game via CrossOver on a MacBook Pro really speaks to how well this game can play on sub-optimal CPUs. The environment is very well designed and detail, the lore is immense, and the music is fantastic. 
As for the gameplay itself, its lovely. The feeling of watching giants fight each other while you rat your way across the battlefield is something I always wanted in a game; not to be the all-powerful force, but to be the runt scurrying around the battlefield. Not to mention to be scared ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ by an EXO around the corner because you decided to be wreckless.
The developers have shown their love with this project to incredible degrees. They are constantly taking feedback and making improvements that make this game more enjoyable. To me, this was a worthwhile purchase for the sheer effort in this masterpiece.

If you have a niche taste of being the little guy in a massive battle, and are patient to endure the fixes, this game is worth your time.

"you are not that guy" interesting hook, I bit.  The literal first thing this game has you do (after running through some hallways with nothing in them but vibes and some very good lighting) is gun down an entire squad of terminator styled cyborgs. Then when I was done doing that, or so I thought, one ran up behind me silently even though the rest of them are loud as ♥♥♥♥ and killed me, which then catapulted me to the hideout that I think I was actually supposed to have discovered on my own. So in the first 20 minutes of gameplay I have encountered a baldfaced lie from the developers as to the core of thier game, and the gsame activley robbed me of what I think should have been my first discovery of the game. This is not good game design. This is in fact, bad game design. Save your money until the the rest of the game catches up to the art.

gave it a run. feels like an odd escape from tarkov. the graphics is trash even at max seetings on 4k im only getting 60fps with a rtx5080 and th game looks like it was made in 2010. the AI is crap you try and hide and then they just get stuck in a loop waiting for you to come out and once you get spotted you start getting shot from everything around the map especially on the surface area. there is no sneak of visibility metre either so how do you guage how stealth you are you cant..... this is not shooter where you try kill everything to survivie its a stealth extraction survial game where you spend 45 minuts trying to takea glass of water back home without spilling it. for how bad this game looks visually i would think a rtx5080 would run this way nicer than 60-80fps @ 4k

The biggest issue for me is the vendor reputation system.  Losing reputation with a vendor for defending yourself on the map and then trying to get that rep back is pain in the ass.  It is the reason I stopped playing.  The maps also have so many objects to get stuck on that it's a chore to traverse the map without constantly getting caught or hung up on some sort of object.

This game tries to do a few impressive things and fails and each and every single one of them. Things are either flawed and don't work, or can be cheesed so much you'll just quit to play another game.

F#$% the haters! This game is sick!! It's in Early Access y'all, keep that in mind. Sure there's some visual bugs and little annoying things. For instance I bought some DLC skins to support the homies, NOW I CAN'T SWITCH BACK!!! Little things like that, you just take with a grain of salt and consider its' source. IT'S EARLY ACCESS FOLKS!!!! Relax. The bones are there, the gameplay loop is amazing and the lore... ah the lore.... the story is great! I hope for more. More skins please!!!!! Cool ones this time. Add some more characters. Overall though, game is dope, between the constant runs and then sellin' off your loot. Doing everything to survive. Upgrading and customizing your weapons. All of it, super satisfying. I just want more. The fleshing out of the more critical things will come through updates with more content and bug fixes. It just needs time in the oven. Let 'em cook people. They got something here and I love it!!!!!!

I thought I would revisit the game since it's been a hot minute since I've played it. It has had mixed reviews since launch but the aesthetic of it is decent enough. At least, that's how it used to be. I could overlook the janky enemy ai. The environments were okay. The loot was satisfying enough. Heck, even the gun physics weren't that shabby. The drama about the water draining in real time wasn't even that big of a deal to me on launch because it really wasn't that hard to stockpile water. Depending on the area, you could reasonably go in, get some loot, and get out before the hunters showed up.

This tunnel system... it sucks... bad. Like, really bad. I'm not in this for jump puzzles or to navigate a maze to even reach my destination. The old load screens were perfectly sufficient as they are in most games. I'm not that die hard to role play "travel from a-z" simulator. If i was, I'd go play Death Stranding.

Bag man was my go-to for the longest time. He packs some insane storage for ammo, can carry some insane firepower, and still have plenty of room to store all the booze and cigarettes. He isn't meant to parkour his way through the tunnels. Every jump feels like I need to clench just to get that extra little bit of air time. Not to mention that some of the navigation in the tunnels evidently expects you to sacrifice health to make it down from a platform onto a pipe as a makeshift bridge. Guess what giant pipes lack - cover. I jump down to this pipe which knocked me from 1800 health down to 600. I had a bunch of medical supplies with me, so I didn't think it was a big deal and started healing up. Well, now I'm on this pipe with NO COVER and a firing squad across the way where I assume I need to go just pissing bullets in my direction. I just wanted to go into the scorched plains, or anywhere for that matter and shoot and loot like I did when the game released, but it's an instant uninstall as long as the tunnels are a thing.

Game is still too glitchy for full release, I keep getting stuck in the most random places and have to return to base. You can't tell your squadmates to stay so they AI just runs into gun fire and dies.  There is a fun game here, but it's not there yet.

The games best feature is it's artstyle and even that doesn't seem to be fully utilized. Most of the maps are just like barren cityscapes that have been destroyed 100 times over, which is what they are. But that's literally everywhere there's not many interesting sites to see.

 The game feels very small in terms of its scale. There's not a lot of verticality in maps which is a shame considering I imagined exploring the world would have, the combat itself isn't very interesting (very simple 3rd person shooting mechanics) and most enemies feel weaker than they look, which I suppose is a good thing. 

TL;DR = interesting game, interesting artstyle, pretty bland and uninspired gameplay, doesn't really deliver on anything. It feels like it wanted to be what Arc Raiders is, but more dystopian and brutalist. Anyway it sucks, don't play.

A game I wish had a mixed review option. But I'll get to it.

What's stopping me from dumping hundreds of hours into this right now is the AI and stealth mechanics. They're not great, I've experienced worse, but the stealth mechanics feel pretty undercooked right now.

It shows promise, but its a tad underbaked. Some runs are stealth, other times you just get instantly spotted, it feels like there's no rhyme or reason to it. They've done some changes I like, some I don't, not enough to get me to dislike the game. I don't even mine the jank animations at times. I just want the mechanics to feel more consistent. 

Once it gets some polish I feel like it'll be great, right now its just a mixed bag.

I want to be clear. I bought this game for the vision of it, for its atmosphere. And i got some absolutely breathtaking moments with it. I bought it on release day and it was harsh. Bugs, some would say unplayable. But the developers are doing a great job, constantly updating the game, bringing in new content and working with the community. 
Two or three times a year i start it to see whats new and to check on the improvements. Its still an Early-Access- Experience but you can see the progress and i hope that eventually it will reach its full potential. Forever Winter is a game with an almost one of a kind artstyle and the gameworld is so detailed. Sometimes i just load into a map to find a nice spot and sit there for a while and enjoy the view and audio. 
If you want to see a world devastated by endless war and manmade horrors, you`ve come to the right place.

This has to be the first and only game that I absolutely hate but don't regret buying.

The Forever Winter was first supposed to be an art book, and then a "living art book" and then a Tarkov-inspired extraction shooter. You'll notice the first part immediately. "Every frame a painting" has perhaps applied to no other game better. You have never seen anything else quite like this and that's the point. The world is incredibly bleak, desolate, ravaged and yet beautiful and captivating all the same.  It's twisted and surreal and yet grounded, a fever-nightmare that still manages to be plausible and relevant to what we're living through today. It's aesthetic and meaning married in a way that only the best art can be.

This is also a world that makes no secret of the fact it hates you, the rest of humanity too, but it really hates you specifically. You're a scavenger in this world, which is a constant active war zone filled with literal man-made horrors beyond your comprehension. You are less than a worm here. This isn't like Tarkov so much as it's like the Hell all the worst Tarkov players go to. With a lot of time and grind you become more survivable, but never on top. This game is in no way meant to be enjoyed so much as endured, the most you can hope for is to just make it through another run. Do I find it fun? No, certainly not. Will I still be coming back to it years from now? Yeah, probably.

I just really hope they keep this IP alive with other media and games. It deserves to be seen by a broader audience.
Amazing art style and atmosphere rivaled by none. There are some issues gameplay wise but the devs are hard at work ironing them out.

Leaving a review just so the devs know their work is appreciated, the game is rough at first but the more you get into it the more enjoyable it gets if you can excuse the bugs/early access state of the game. What brings me back mainly is the aesthetic of the game, its like a perfect blend of Ashley Wood and Zdzisław Beksiński style. Hope to see this game have a long run as it really is something special

The atmosphere, the lore, and the overall IDEA around this universe is absolutely perfect in every way shape or form. The gameplay loop, content within, and mechanics of the game are slowly getting better with every update. If you put this game down, I recommend picking it up again or purchasing to support the devs. There's something really special here it just needs  more time to cook.

Amazing atmosphere and really get the feel of you being the little guy. Even with the game in EA the game has enough meat to keep you going for a long time. There will be occasional jank, which is to be expected.

Hard, but thrilling to survive in these fascinating settings.
Great artwork and impressive progress in developing this game so far.
Wonderfully crafted scavenging game, devs are very active and post regular updates and new content as well.
Love the world, unreasonably good for being in early access, so much potential

This game has a lot going for it; ambiance, good graphics, a grim post-apocalypse world, and a story I'm interested in.  Definitely recommend it for someone who enjoys challenging, survival, extraction games set in a gritty universe. FunDog Studios has something special here, and I hope they continue to hold true to what they've started and expand this dark and gritty world while doing their best in keeping true to their core message of "you aren't that guy" (at least at first, lol).

Now, my initial review was very critical of the early game, but after putting a dozen or so hours into the environment of Lost Angels, I have found my rhythm and cadence with the game, though I have a couple of things I hope get addressed by Fun Dog Studios.  
Firstly, one thing that has been plaguing my game occasionally is that my controls will lock up.  I like to swap between a controller and KB&M, and there are times where my inputs aren't being detected/received/etc by the game itself; it's not a PC issue because I don't have this problem with any other game, period.
Secondly, I respect the decision to have co-op/multiplayer be P2P, that saves on the cost and time for servers and ensures the game can be multiplayer well into the future.  HOWEVER, I have had OCCASIONAL difficulties with this system where I will be having a really good run and then either lose connection or have my game bug out.
I believe these issues are completely resolvable though and Fun Dog Studios just needs to shift more focus from putting out new content and instead make the necessary repairs to the core game experience and make sure the foundation of this growing world is as solid as possible.  I am NOT a game developer, but I feel that enough content has been released, that the primary focus now should be optimization, bug squashing, and stability, which will help the current community greatly and make it easier for new scavs to dip their toes into this game with so much potential.
